era_context: Late Bronze Age, Egypt's 18th Dynasty under Amenhotep III. The Mycenaean world is in LHIIIA; the Hittite Empire is expanding; Mitanni is in decline. Shang Dynasty in China continues.

events.title: Amenhotep III's reign; continued prosperity and construction
events.description: Amenhotep III's reign continues. Egypt remains the dominant power in the eastern Mediterranean and Nubia. The pharaoh continues to commission construction projects and maintain diplomatic relations with neighboring powers. This period is characterized by Egypt's economic and military dominance.

sources.name: Temple inscriptions and administrative records from Egypt

events.certainty_note: Amenhotep III's reign is well-attested. The year 1376 BCE is assigned by conventional dating with ±5–10 year uncertainty.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are well-attested for the year 1376 BCE. The assignment of Amenhotep III's reign to this year is based on conventional chronology, which is debated among Egyptologists.
historiographic_note: For this year, no specific events are documented in surviving sources. The broader context of Amenhotep III's reign is well-known, but individual years within his reign are often not distinguished in the historical record.
